ads.author: Steve Hayslip
ads.title: 1961 Gottlieb Foto Finish Pinball Machine
ads.description: 1961 Gottlieb “Foto Finish” Pinball Machine – Original – Complete – Franklin, TN For sale is an original 1961 Gottlieb Foto Finish electromechanical pinball machine. This machine has been in our family for years and was working when it was placed into storage several years ago. It is currently not playing a game, although the lights come on when powered up. Because of that, I’m selling it as-is. Since it worked before storage, I believe it may just need the typical cleaning, adjustment, or servicing that older electromechanical machines often require after sitting. Overall, it appears to be a very complete, original machine. Missing the key to access the back panel, but front panel IS open and accessible. Condition: ● Original back glass with bright, attractive artwork ● Original playfield in nice condition for its age ● Lights power on ● Cabinet has normal cosmetic wear and paint loss consistent with a 65-year-old machine ● Sold as-is, with no warranty This would make a great restoration project or addition to a vintage pinball collection. Price: $1,200 OBO Located in Franklin, Tennessee. This machine is very heavy, so local pickup only. You’ll want to bring at least one helper and a suitable vehicle.

ads.author: Alan E Gutberlet
ads.title: Restored Midway Pac-man Arcade
ads.description: If you’ve been looking for an original Midway Pac-man upright video arcade game in great condition, this is the one! Originally released in 1980 and responsible for the start of the video arcade game revolution! Own a very nice example, cabinet stenciled artwork is original, with minor touchup to cabinet and presents very well. Cabinet itself is solid with no water damage. Notice the original coin door has no rust, dents, or scratches, both coin slots work, no lock bar holes in the front kick panel. Monitor glass bezel is in great condition with no scratches on the glass, includes the speed chip if you prefer to have Pacman run faster - currently set as game was originally delivered from the factory. A really nice condition Pacman for your game room or mancave - own a piece of Arcade History! Key things done during the restoration: 1. New Cabinet Rear Door 2. CRT monitor was serviced earlier this year including replacement of ALL Electrolytic capacitors for trouble free operation. CRT is burn-free, sharp, and bright as indicated in the photos - one of the nicest I’ve seen. 3. New leg levelers 4. new T-moulding in the original orange color 5. Refurbishment of control panel and joystick switches - including new control panel overlay graphics 6. New coin door and back door locks 7. Refurbishment of coin door 8. Replacement of Marquee incandescent bulbs with LED bulbs for long life Before you ask; the Playboy pinball pictured next to Pac-man is not for sale at this time.
